steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor
steps.content: Each side of parallelogram JKLM is 3 times the corresponding side of WXYZ. The linear scale factor is $k = 3$.

steps.title: Apply area scaling for similar figures
steps.content:
When similar figures have a linear scale factor of $k$, their areas scale by $k^2$. Multiply the original area by the area scale factor:
$$4 \times 3^2 = 4 \times 9 = 36 \text{ cm}^2$$
That is the correct answer.
